mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kubeflow is a powerful machine learning toolkit for Kubernetes that provides a comprehensive set of tools for building and deploying AI applications. Installing Kubeflow enables you to leverage its modular and scalable architecture to support various stages of the AI lifecycle.

steps.title: Add the Kubeflow Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Kubeflow Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add kubeflow https://charts.kubeflow.org
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kubeflow using Helm
steps.description:
Next, install Kubeflow with the specified version in a new namespace called 'kubeflow'.
```bash
helm install kubeflow kubeflow/kubeflow --version v1.10.0 --namespace kubeflow --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all the pods are running in the 'kubeflow' namespace to ensure a successful installation.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kubeflow
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, configure resource limits for the Kubeflow components. You can edit the Helm release to set resource limits as needed.
```bash
helm upgrade kubeflow kubeflow/kubeflow --namespace kubeflow --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=1Gi
```

steps.title: Access the Kubeflow dashboard
steps.description:
Finally, set up port forwarding to access the Kubeflow dashboard locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/istio-ingressgateway -n kubeflow 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kubeflow will show all pods in the 'kubeflow' namespace as running. You can access the Kubeflow dashboard by navigating to http://localhost:8080 in your web browser.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubeflow https://charts.kubeflow.org
helm repo update
helm install kubeflow kubeflow/kubeflow --version v1.10.0 --namespace kubeflow --create-namespace
helm upgrade kubeflow kubeflow/kubeflow --namespace kubeflow --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=1Gi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed.
